sections.title: Problems
items: History of Breast Cancer (in remission 2 years)

data.to: Hepatology Department, Milton Keynes University Hospital
data.reason_for_referral: New onset mildly deranged LFTs in a patient with history of breast cancer (in remission 2 years). Recent onset vague abdominal discomfort, fatigue, and loss of appetite. Concerned about possible hepatic metastasis.
relevant_past_medical_history: Breast Cancer — Stage IIA, ER+/HER2-, treated with lumpectomy + radiotherapy + anastrozole. In remission 2 years.

data.chief_complaint: Vague abdominal discomfort for past 3 weeks. Feeling more tired than usual. Reduced appetite. Has lost about 2kg but attributes to eating less.
data.hpi: 60F with history of breast cancer in remission. New onset non-specific abdominal symptoms. Concerned about cancer recurrence. No jaundice, no dark urine, no night sweats.

physical_examination.general: Slim female, appears mildly fatigued. No distress.
physical_examination.abdomen: Soft, mild diffuse tenderness on deep palpation. No masses. No organomegaly.
physical_examination.skin: No jaundice. Well-healed surgical scar left breast.

data.assessment: New LFT derangement in context of breast cancer history is concerning. ALP rise could indicate bone or hepatic metastasis. Needs further investigation — imaging + hepatology review.

sections.title: Pre-Consultation Questionnaire
qa: Vague tummy discomfort for a few weeks. Tired. Not eating as much. Lost a bit of weight but not trying to.
About 1 unit per week. Occasional glass of wine.
Anastrozole, Amlodipine, Alendronic acid, Calcium + Vit D
